senses.definition: a journey for pleasure, during which we visit several different places

examples.x: He booked a tour to explore the best surf spots on the island.
The couple embarked on a tour across Europe to celebrate their anniversary.
They planned a three-day tour to explore the historical sites in Rome.
We joined a guided tour to learn more about the national park's wildlife.
We took a bike tour through the countryside, enjoying the serene landscapes.

senses.definition: ‌the business of providing accommodation, services and entertainment for people who are visiting a place for pleasure

examples.x: Government policies are encouraging the growth of rural tourism.
The natural beauty of the island makes it a hot spot for tourism.
The new amusement park is expected to boost tourism in the city.
The rise in adventure tourism has led to the development of various outdoor activities.
The tourism industry has been impacted significantly by global travel restrictions.

senses.definition: someone who visits a place or travels to different places for pleasure

examples.x: As a tourist in Paris, she made sure to visit the Louvre Museum.
Even though he was a tourist, he tried to blend in with the local culture.
She worked as a tour guide, assisting tourists in understanding the history of the city.
The tourist appreciated the local art displayed in the city's public spaces.
Tourists took several photos of the picturesque landscape.

senses.definition: the activity of visiting interesting places in a particular location as a tourist

examples.x: Our vacation itinerary included two days of sightseeing in Barcelona.
She was exhausted after a full day of sightseeing in Paris.
The city offers a wide range of sightseeing opportunities, from historic monuments to modern art galleries.
The travel agency organized a sightseeing tour of the most popular landmarks in the city.
Their sightseeing in London included the Tower of London, the British Museum, and Buckingham Palace.

senses.definition: a person whose job is to take tourists to interesting places and show them around

examples.x: During our trip to the vineyard, the guide explained the process of wine making.
Our guide for the castle tour had interesting stories about the royal family.
She works as a guide at the local museum, explaining the history behind each artifact.
The guide at the art gallery provided insightful commentary on the artworks.
The knowledgeable museum guide made the history exhibits come alive.

senses.definition: someone traveling in a vehicle, aircraft, ship, etc. who is not the pilot, driver, or a crew member

examples.x: She often reads a book to pass the time when she's a passenger on long road trips.
The bus passenger pressed the button to request the next stop.
The bus was crowded with passengers during the morning commute.
The flight attendant demonstrated safety procedures to the passengers.
The passenger on the cruise ship enjoyed a view of the ocean from her cabin.

senses.definition: a person who is on a journey or someone who travels a lot

examples.x: As a solo traveler, she appreciated the freedom to create her own itinerary.
As an avid traveler, he has visited over 50 countries.
Despite being a seasoned traveler, she still felt a thrill boarding the plane.
The traveler explored the local market for souvenirs.
The traveler navigated the city streets with the help of a map.

senses.definition: a case with a handle, used for carrying clothes, etc. when we are traveling

examples.x: She always puts a colorful tag on her suitcase to easily identify it at baggage claim.
She packed her suitcase carefully, making sure to include all her essentials.
The child sat on top of the suitcase while his dad tried to zip it closed.
The handle of his suitcase broke as he was rushing to catch his flight.
The traveler struggled with his heavy suitcase up the stairs.

senses.definition: suitcases or other bags, containing our clothes and things, that we carry when we are traveling

examples.x: After a mix-up at the airport, he ended up with someone else's baggage.
During the security check, they asked her to open her baggage for inspection.
He had to pay extra for his baggage because it was over the weight limit.
He stored his winter clothes in the baggage during the summer trip.
The airline lost my baggage during the transfer, but they delivered it to my hotel the next day.

senses.definition: the place or desk usually at a hotel entrance where people go to book a room or check in

examples.x: On checking out, he returned his room key to the reception.
She requested an extra towel at the reception.
The receptionist at the reception was very helpful in guiding us to nearby attractions.
They left their luggage at the reception until their room was ready.
They requested a room with a sea view at the reception.

senses.definition: one of a pair of single beds in a hotel or guest room for two people

examples.x: Her dorm room was small, with just enough space for twin beds and a desk.
The beach cottage they rented for the summer had a room with twin beds.
The guest room has twin beds, perfect for when his friends come to visit.
The host had arranged the twin beds neatly before their arrival.
The twin beds were covered with colorful linens, adding a cheerful touch to the room.

senses.definition: a bed that is designed for one person

examples.x: He chose a hotel room with a single bed since he was traveling alone.
Her dormitory had a single bed, a study table, and a wardrobe.
The camping cabin was basic, with a single bed and a chair.
The child's room was decorated with a single bed and colorful wallpaper.
The single bed in the cabin was narrow but surprisingly comfortable.

senses.definition: a hotel room or bedroom used by just one person

examples.x: He preferred staying in a single room while traveling for privacy.
She booked a single room in the hotel for her business trip.
She got a great deal on a single room at the beach resort.
The single room had a beautiful view of the city skyline.
The single room in the hostel was small but comfortable.

senses.definition: a room in a hotel suitable for two people, typically has a larger bed

examples.x: She loved the double room's large windows that let in plenty of natural light.
She requested a double room on the ground floor due to her mobility issues.
The double room was equipped with air conditioning and a flat-screen TV.
The students shared a double room in the dormitory to save on rent.
Their double room was just steps away from the sandy beach.

senses.definition: ‌a company or business that provides air transportation services for people and goods

examples.x: He always flies with the same airline to accumulate loyalty points.
She chose the airline because of its reputation for excellent customer service.
She works as a flight attendant for a major airline.
The airline announced a new route to Tokyo starting next month.
The airline offers daily flights from New York to London.

senses.definition: a scheduled journey by an aircraft

examples.x: He managed to catch some sleep during the long flight.
His next flight was a connecting one in Amsterdam.
She missed her flight and had to book another one.
The flight across the Atlantic took about seven hours.

senses.definition: a part of an airport or terminal that passengers go through to get on or off a plane, train, or bus

examples.x: He read a book while waiting at the gate for his flight.
The flight was delayed, so they had to spend a few hours at the gate.
The gate closed shortly after the final boarding call.
The gate for the next flight was already bustling with passengers.
They had a long walk between gates to catch their connecting flight.

senses.definition: happening in or between more than one country

examples.x: He is an international student studying in a university abroad.
She is a correspondent for an international news agency.
The international airport serves flights to and from various countries around the world.
The international school offers a curriculum that is recognized worldwide.
They hosted an international art exhibition showcasing works from around the world.

senses.definition: a place in a plane, train, theater, etc. that is designed for people to sit on, particularly one requiring a ticket

examples.x: He checked his ticket to find the correct seat in the stadium.
He chose a seat near the exit for a quick departure after the event.
She found an empty seat on the bus and settled in for the journey.
She was lucky to find a seat on the crowded train.
The seat in the airplane was equipped with a small fold-down table.

senses.definition: a ticket or card that passengers must show to be allowed on a ship or plane

examples.x: He forgot his boarding pass in the waiting area and had to rush back to get it.
She confirmed her frequent flyer number was on the boarding pass to earn miles.
She was relieved when she found her boarding pass in her coat pocket.
The airline sent her a digital boarding pass to her email after online check-in.
The boarding pass was required for the tax refund process at the airport.

senses.definition: a ticket that can be used for travelling to a place and coming back from that place

examples.x: He lost his round-trip ticket and had to buy a new one.
She bought a round-trip ticket for her vacation to Spain.
She checked the dates on her round-trip ticket to make sure they were correct.
The price of the round-trip ticket for the concert bus included a discount.
The travel agency offered a package deal including hotel and round-trip ticket.

senses.definition: a ticket that can be used for travelling to a place but cannot be used for coming back from that place

examples.x: He bought a one-way ticket to New York to start his new job.
He found a great deal on a one-way ticket for his flight to Tokyo.
She purchased a one-way ticket for the train, not knowing when she'd return.
The cost of a one-way ticket for the cable car was well worth the view.
The one-way ticket for the express bus was more expensive, but saved time.

senses.definition: to reserve a specific thing such as a seat, ticket, hotel room, etc.

examples.x: I need to book a flight to New York for next week's business meeting.
She booked a hotel room in advance to ensure she had a place to stay during her vacation.
The concert tickets were selling out quickly, so I hurried to book mine online.
They decided to book a table at their favorite restaurant for their anniversary dinner.
We should book our seats for the movie premiere as soon as possible to avoid missing out.

senses.definition: the system of vehicles, such as buses, trains, etc. that are available to everyone and provided by the government or companies

examples.x: He fell asleep on the public transportation and missed his stop.
She had a map of the public transportation routes to navigate the city.
She uses public transportation daily to commute to work.
The hotel was conveniently located near several public transportation stops.
The public transportation options in the city are affordable and reliable.

senses.definition: the raised surface in a station next to a railroad track where people can get on and off a train

examples.x: He checked the platform number on his ticket before heading towards his train.
She stood at the edge of the platform, watching the incoming train.
The announcement indicated that the train to Berlin would arrive at platform 3.
The platform was well-lit and had seating for waiting passengers.
The train pulled into the platform, and the passengers began to board.

senses.definition: a system or network of tracks with the trains, organization, and people needed to operate them

examples.x: He works as an engineer for the local railroad company.
The railroad connects the city center with the suburban areas.
The railroad construction project was expected to be completed by next year.
The scenic railroad tour offered stunning views of the mountains.

senses.definition: the amount of money we pay to travel with a bus, taxi, plane, etc.

examples.x: She bought a monthly pass to save on daily fare expenses.
She checked the fare on the ride-hailing app before confirming her trip.
The airline offers lower fares for early morning flights.
The fare from the city to the beach was quite reasonable.

senses.definition: a fixed way between two places, along which a bus, plane, ship, etc. regularly travels

examples.x: She looked at the route map before her road trip.
The airline launched a new route to Tokyo last month.
The bus followed its usual route despite the heavy snow.
The cruise ship followed a route along the Mediterranean coast.

senses.definition: to travel in a vehicle such as a bus, car, etc.

examples.x: He prefers to ride the bus to work instead of driving.
I ride the subway to school every day.
In the crowded city, it's often more convenient to ride a taxi than to navigate public transportation.
As a tourist in the city, she chose to ride a double-decker sightseeing bus to explore the famous landmarks.

senses.definition: to reach and get on a bus, aircraft, or train in time
to fail to catch a bus, airplane, etc.

examples.x: I got lost on my way to the airport and I'm going to miss my flight.
I missed my flight because I arrived at the airport too late.
She missed the school bus because she forgot her backpack.
She overslept and missed her morning bus to work.
She was so engrossed in her book that she missed her metro stop.

senses.definition: to meet and greet someone who has just arrived

examples.x: He welcomed his friend at the train station who was visiting from another city.
The fans waited outside the stadium to welcome their favorite band.
The students organized a party to welcome the new exchange student.
The tour guide welcomed the tourists at the hotel lobby.
They went to the airport to welcome their relatives from abroad.

senses.definition: a passage used for walking, riding, or driving

examples.x: The narrow way led to the village.
They strolled down the quiet way.
His car was parked along the main way.
